content:
Just now

--

JavaScript
Every time JavaScript runs code or calls a function, it creates an execution context (EC)
There are two phases

• Memory creation phase

• var -> undefined

• let / const -> hoisted (Temporal Dead Zone)

• function declarations -> stored completely
2. Execution phase

• code runs line by line

• variables get assigned values

• function calls create new Execution Context
Every function call gets its own execution context and its own memory. when the function finishes, its execution context is removed from the call stack.
## A moment of pain is worth a lifetime of glory. ##
